entries.garrytan/gbrain: This project functions as a self-wiring knowledge graph that uses automated entity extraction and typed linking to organize agent intelligence. It distinguishes itself through a hybrid search system that combines vector retrieval with backlink-boosted ranking and twenty-six specialized skills for data enrichment.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It is designed to help agents build evolving mental models and integrates seamlessly with major providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a multi-agent loop to propose, analyze, and curate knowledge for complex tasks. It specializes in self-improvement, distilling validated execution data into optimized runtimes to enhance agent behavior over repeated scenario runs.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A Lossless Context Management plugin that utilizes a hierarchical DAG-based engine to ensure no message is ever lost during compaction. It features three-level escalation logic and immutable message persistence within profile-scoped SQLite databases for structured history retrieval.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This backend provides a private, self-hosted solution for cross-session memory persistence using Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis. It is ideal for users prioritizing data sovereignty, supporting any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that uses a unified SQLite vault to manage memory across different agents without cloud dependencies. It employs a sophisticated hybrid retrieval strategy combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal to automate decision capture and handoffs.
entries.plur-ai/plur: This shared memory layer utilizes an open YAML 'engram' format to store persistent data locally with zero API costs. It features a unique biological approach to memory management, where information automatically decays or strengthens based on its usage frequency.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches relevant context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. Built as an MCP-native server, it leverages SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide low-latency RAG capabilities for coding agent clients.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This project provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns through a tiered word replacement system and a two-pass verification process. It is specifically designed to help Hermes Agent produce more human-like, undetectable prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular cross-platform library that equips agents with frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation of specialized professional skillsets into any agentskills.io-compatible environment.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This library implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety and efficiency. It uses progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption during skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams, including UML and architecture maps, directly from natural language. It features grid-aligned layouts and specialized tensor shape annotations for machine learning visualizations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Focused on blockchain interoperability, this project provides specialized skills for interacting with Chainlink's Oracle networks and CCIP. It allows Hermes to perform on-chain operations and smart contract interactions using the official Agent Skills specification.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill introduces literate programming capabilities, allowing Hermes to weave runnable code and documentation into unified markdown files. It includes a 'tangler' to extract source files and automatically syncs edits between code and prose.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with a shared memory system and a pipeline for training its own replacement models. It extracts decision-quality data from active workflows to facilitate fine-tuning and cross-instance knowledge persistence.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A comprehensive registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with built-in monetization via Stripe and TON. It automates dependency resolution and provides a Python-based CLI for local skill installation.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: This is the official integration for FLUX image generation, providing Hermes with standardized prompting guidelines and API patterns. It supports precise visual control through JSON structured prompting and hex color specifications.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill acts as a workflow observer that automatically detects and proposes new, reusable skills based on the agent's activity. It generates both the necessary Python scaffolds and documentation to turn repetitive tasks into permanent capabilities.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness for long-running missions that utilizes structured memory and validation assertions. It enables Hermes to manage complex plans with milestones and perform clean handoffs between agent sessions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and real-time progress tracking. It ensures operational safety by providing rollback procedures to recover from errors during complex workflows.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive Model Context Protocol client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token usage. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading of configurations.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This tool converts source code and developer documentation into verified, version-pinned skills for AI agents. It links every instruction to specific file paths and line numbers to ensure high-fidelity function calling.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community-driven hub for browsing and installing Hermes skills with integrated security scanning for prompt injections. It supports micropayments for premium skills and features an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scores.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, allowing C# developers to integrate Hermes with MCP. It supports automatic skill discovery and uses progressive disclosure to optimize context window efficiency.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages for a wide array of AI coding agents, including Hermes. It allows for direct tool execution via Nix flake commands, making it a versatile choice for users managing multiple agent environments.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw offers a high-security deployment by utilizing hardware-level enforcement to maintain strict network, filesystem, and process boundaries. It includes three security policy presets and supports over 40 built-in tools, including MCP integration.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: Designed for reproducibility, this NixOS module enables declarative management of agent configurations, documents, and systemd services. It ensures secure secrets handling through sops-nix integration and supports both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This Windows-based solution provides a no-install, no-Docker deployment that requires no administrative rights. It features a GUI, 100 integrated tools, local model management, and a workflow engine for scheduled or conditional automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a straightforward Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. Users can maintain persistent state through mounted home directories and build specific Hermes tags using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Tailored for production environments on Ubuntu and Debian, this headless deployment configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integrates with the Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment utilizes a Docker Compose stack that integrates directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack. It features a ttyd web terminal interface and automates image builds via GitHub Actions and GHCR.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen with zero external dependencies. It is essential for Hermes users who require a private environment to monitor task spend and implement human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that support 23 different model providers, including Hermes and local LLMs. It distinguishes itself through persistent agent memory and a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism that expands agent capabilities over time.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to improve the performance of Hermes Agent on benchmarks. It orchestrates structured candidate mutations to find the most efficient harness code for TBLite and TB2 evaluation tasks.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars in real-time. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics through a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases ranging from research and planning to infrastructure. It ensures high-quality outputs by implementing mandatory quality gates and code reviews at every stage of the multi-agent lifecycle.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It enforces architectural integrity through an eight-phase process where each transition is protected by automated graph-diff phase gates.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project enables ACP-style delegation by routing tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It prioritizes safety and efficiency through isolated workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It leverages LiteLLM pricing data to provide developers with detailed 3D visualizations and usage analytics for their local and cloud-based workflows.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal experience through custom YAML-based themes and ASCII banners. It features a flexible inheritance system that lets users override specific UI elements like spinners and color schemes while maintaining the core interface functionality.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth. It is particularly valuable for developers requiring native MLOps capabilities, such as vLLM inference management and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, Vessel exposes structured page schemas and durable session states via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It bridges the gap between agents and the web by providing a supervisory sidebar for human auditing and manual intervention during complex workflows.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md introduces a scoped framework for Hermes, allowing developers to define project-specific memories, tools, and instructions within local folders. It enables seamless switching between global settings and specialized agent configurations tailored to the unique requirements of a single workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static analysis tool identifies structural flaws and ambiguities in agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM API costs. By detecting failure modes like tool loops, it serves as an automated quality gate for integrating Hermes agents into CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes by mapping complex model configurations and personas into the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of workspace files, credentials, and memories to ensure a consistent agent experience across platforms.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment stack uses Docker to orchestrate a complete Hermes environment featuring LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local memory. It is optimized for zero-cost operations, providing 29 pre-configured plugins and automated routing for free model inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-framework interface provides a mobile-responsive three-panel layout for seamless chat, file browsing, and session management. It maintains full parity with the Hermes CLI, offering a lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript alternative for users who want the complete agent experience in a browser.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A security-hardened workspace that features real-time SSE streaming and integrated rendering for tool calls. It excels at scale, providing specialized interfaces to manage over 2,000 skills alongside a dedicated memory browser and execution approval prompts.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway, allowing users to start, stop, and configure multiple profiles from a single self-hosted hub. It stands out for its detailed token analytics, which track costs across different models and platforms in real-time.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that utilizes direct SSH connections to interact with Hermes without requiring local file mirroring or background daemons. It features a profile-aware workspace and a multi-tabbed terminal with customizable themes for a professional developer experience.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed for the Telegram Mini App ecosystem, featuring a terminal-style chat with slash command support. It allows users to spawn background agents and monitor system health through live CPU, memory, and disk usage gauges.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process by providing guided installation and dependency resolution for the Hermes Agent. It serves as a versatile bridge, supporting various LLM providers and integrating messaging gateways for platforms like Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on observability, this dashboard provides real-time activity monitoring and cost distribution tracking for Hermes sessions. It includes FTS5 search capabilities for inspecting conversation history and a read-only viewer for configuration and cron job management.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that integrates a marketplace for skills, MCP servers, and plugins into the agent workflow. It provides isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate while visualizing tool execution on a live timeline.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic web interface uses a unique single-file React architecture that requires no build step for self-hosting. It features an artifact panel for live rendering of HTML and SVG outputs alongside real-time SSE log streaming for tool call visualization.
